cartaIssuerId.description: Carta issuer ID associated with this company.

cartaCorporationId.description: Carta corporation ID associated with this company.

advisers.description: Array of contact IDs linked as advisers to the deal.

introducer.description: Array of contact IDs linked as introducers to the deal.

management.description: Array of contact IDs linked as management to the deal.

people.description: Contacts linked to the deal by role (advisers, introducer, management). Note: these fields may have different names on the UI.

dealLead.description: User ID to assign as deal lead

fields.description: Custom field values keyed by field ID. For fields of type `company`, provide either a company ID or a domain string (e.g. `"stripe.com"`). For `company-list` fields, provide an array of company IDs and/or domain strings. If a domain matches an existing company in your organization, the company record is linked directly; otherwise the domain is resolved via external enrichment. For fields of type `deal`, provide a deal ID. For `contacts-list` fields, provide an array of contact IDs.

people.description: Contacts linked to the deal by role (advisers, introducer, management). Note: these fields may have different names on the UI.

deals.description: Array of deals to create. Maximum 100 items per request.

dealLead.description: User ID to assign as deal lead

If a domain matches an existing company in your organization, the company record is linked directly; otherwise the domain is resolved via external enrichment. For fields of type `deal`, provide a deal ID. For `contacts-list` fields, provide an array of contact IDs.

id.description: ID of the deal to update.

deals.description: Array of deals to update. Each item must include an `id` field. Maximum 100 items per request.

fields.description: Custom field values keyed by field ID. For fields of type `company`, provide either a company ID or a domain string (e.g. `"stripe.com"`). For `company-list` fields, provide an array of company IDs and/or domain strings. For fields of type `deal`, provide a deal ID. For `contacts-list` fields, provide an array of contact IDs.

location.description: Fully replaces the work location when provided.

homeLocation.description: Fully replaces the home location when provided.

jobs.description: Fully replaces the jobs array when provided.

socialLinks.description: Partially updates social links — only provided fields are overwritten.

formatting.description: Formatting type for the field value. Allowed values: number, percent, string, boolean, date.

decimalPoints.description: Number of decimal points for number/percent formatting.

suffix.description: Suffix to append to the displayed value.

fieldId.description: Unique identifier of the field used for business logic. Must be unique within the entity.

label.description: Display name of the field shown in the UI.

category.description: Category used for visual grouping of fields in the UI.

type.description: Field type that determines business logic behavior. Allowed values: input, textarea, dropdown, datepicker, multiselect, users-array, contacts-list, checkbox, number, formula, deal, company, company-list, attachment, url-link.

default.description: Default value for the field. Varies by field type. Examples: empty string ("") for "input", empty array ([]) for "multiselect", false for "checkbox".

options.description: Additional configuration for field types that support predefined values, such as "multiselect", "dropdown". Defines the list of selectable options for these field types.

invisibleField.description: Whether the field should be hidden in the UI.

invisibleLabel.description: Whether the field label should be hidden in the UI.

leftMenu.description: Whether the field should appear in the left menu navigation.

mandatory.description: Whether the field is required to be populated.

formula.description: Formula expression for fields of type "formula". Ignored for other field types.

extraConfig.description: Extra configuration for field formatting and display. Applicable to field types that support value formatting, such as "formula", "number", and others. Controls how the field value is rendered (e.g. number of decimal points, suffix, date format).

externalServiceFieldPath.description: Field path used to map data from an external service response. Supports dot notation for nested properties (e.g. "employees.current_count"). Must be used together with externalServiceType. Defaults to "NONE" if not provided.

externalServiceType.description: External service used for automatic field enrichment. The target entity must have the corresponding external service integration enabled, otherwise the field will not be enriched. Must be used together with externalServiceFieldPath. Defaults to "NONE" if not provided. Allowed values: APOLLO, PITCHBOOK, GAIN_PRO, NONE, SELF, CARTA, CARTA_DWH.

readonly.description: Whether the field is read-only and cannot be edited by users.

condition.description: Conditional expression that controls field visibility. When set, the field is only shown if the condition evaluates to true. Defaults to empty string (no condition) if not provided.

type.description: Field type that determines business logic behavior. Allowed values: input, dropdown, multiselect, checkbox.

label.description: Display name of the field shown in the UI.

leftMenu.description: Whether the field should appear in the left menu navigation.

mandatory.description: Whether the field is required to be populated.

externalServiceType.description: External service used for automatic field enrichment. The target entity must have the corresponding external service integration enabled, otherwise the field will not be enriched. Must be used together with externalServiceFieldPath. Defaults to "NONE" if not provided. Allowed values: APOLLO, PITCHBOOK, GAIN_PRO, NONE, SELF, CARTA, CARTA_DWH.

stages.description:
Filter by stage IDs. Stage IDs can be found in GET /v1/deals/pipelines endpoint (stages array within each pipeline).

**Note:** To provide multiple values, repeat the parameter in query: `?stages=value1&stages=value2`

**Note:** To provide multiple values, repeat the parameter in query: `?pipelines=value1&pipelines=value2`

info.title: Carta CRM
info.description:
Carta CRM Public API provides programmatic access to your data.  Use this API to integrate Carta CRM with your internal systems, automate workflows, and build custom reporting solutions.

All endpoints require API key authentication via the Authorization header.

200.description: List of pipelines with their stages

get.description: A **pipeline** represents the sequence of **stages** a deal passes through from initiation to completion. The Deal and Credit teams use different **pipelines** to manage their workflows effectively.

200.description: Schema of custom fields for deals

get.description: **Custom fields** allow users to store additional information alongside a deal. EBITDA, revenue multiple, and other financial metrics can be tracked using **custom fields**.

post.description: Adds a new **custom field** to the deal schema.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **custom field** in the deal schema. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **custom field** from the deal schema.

post.description: Adds a new **stage** to a deal pipeline.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **stage** in a deal pipeline. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **stage** from a deal pipeline.

schema.description:
Filter by stage IDs. Stage IDs can be found in GET /v1/deals/pipelines endpoint (stages array within each pipeline).

**Note:** To provide multiple values, repeat the parameter in query: `?stages=value1&stages=value2`

Filter by pipeline IDs. Pipeline IDs can be found in GET /v1/deals/pipelines endpoint.

**Note:** To provide multiple values, repeat the parameter in query: `?pipelines=value1&pipelines=value2`
Search query string to filter results.
Maximum number of results to return per request.
Number of results to skip for pagination.

200.description: List of deals matching the search criteria

get.description:
Return **deals** based on search criteria, with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

If the pipeline is not specified, the one marked as the default pipeline is used. To search in other pipelines, you need to explicitly pass the `pipelineId`. To search by stages that are not in the default pipeline, you also need to explicitly specify the `pipelineId`.

post.description:
Creates a new deal in the specified pipeline and stage. The deal will be associated with the authenticated organization.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To enable automatic company data enrichment, provide a valid company website URL in the `company.url` field. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

get.description: Returns a single **deal** by its unique identifier.

patch.description:
Partially updates an existing deal. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified; all other fields remain unchanged.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To trigger automatic company data enrichment, update the `company.url` field with a valid company website URL. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

delete.description: Permanently removes a deal from the system.

post.description:
Creates multiple deals in a single request (up to 100). Each deal follows the same rules as the single create endpoint.

**Atomicity:** This operation is all-or-nothing. If any deal in the array fails validation or creation, the entire request returns an error. Deals that were processed before the failure may have already been written; on error, check for partial side effects.

404.description: One or more deals not found

patch.description:
Partially updates multiple deals in a single request (up to 100). Each item must include an `id` field identifying the deal to update. Only the fields provided will be modified.

**Atomicity:** This operation is all-or-nothing. If any deal in the array fails (e.g. deal not found, invalid field), the entire request returns an error.

400.description: Invalid field type or missing file

post.summary: Upload Attachment to Deal Custom Field
post.description: Uploads a file attachment to a deal custom field of type `attachment`. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`.

delete.summary: Delete Attachment from Deal Custom Field
delete.description: Removes a file attachment from a deal custom field of type `attachment`. The file is also deleted from storage.

post.description: Uploads a logo for a deal. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`. Replaces any existing logo.

delete.description: Removes the logo from a deal.

400.description: Invalid field type or missing file

post.summary: Upload Attachment to Investor Custom Field
post.description: Uploads a file attachment to an investor custom field of type `attachment`. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`.

delete.summary: Delete Attachment from Investor Custom Field
delete.description: Removes a file attachment from an investor custom field of type `attachment`. The file is also deleted from storage.

post.description: Uploads a logo for an investor. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`. Replaces any existing logo.

delete.description: Removes the logo from an investor.

400.description: Invalid field type or missing file

post.summary: Upload Attachment to Company Custom Field
post.description: Uploads a file attachment to a company custom field of type `attachment`. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`.

delete.summary: Delete Attachment from Company Custom Field
delete.description: Removes a file attachment from a company custom field of type `attachment`. The file is also deleted from storage.

post.description: Uploads a logo for a company. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`. Replaces any existing logo.

delete.description: Removes the logo from a company.

400.description: Invalid field type or missing file

post.summary: Upload Attachment to Fundraising Custom Field
post.description: Uploads a file attachment to a fundraising custom field of type `attachment`. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`.

delete.summary: Delete Attachment from Fundraising Custom Field
delete.description: Removes a file attachment from a fundraising custom field of type `attachment`. The file is also deleted from storage.

post.description: Uploads a logo for a fundraising opportunity. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`. Replaces any existing logo.

delete.description: Removes the logo from a fundraising opportunity.

get.description: Returns all available fundraising stages. Stages represent the lifecycle phases of a fundraising process.

200.description: Schema of custom fields for fundraising

get.description: Returns the schema of all custom fields configured for the Fundraising entity. Custom fields allow organizations to extend the default fundraising data model with additional attributes.

post.description: Adds a new **custom field** to the fundraising schema.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **custom field** in the fundraising schema. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **custom field** from the fundraising schema.

post.description: Adds a new **stage** to the fundraising lifecycle.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **stage** in the fundraising lifecycle. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **stage** from the fundraising lifecycle.

get.description: Returns a single **fundraising** record by its unique identifier.

patch.description:
Partially updates an existing fundraising record. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified; all other fields remain unchanged.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To trigger automatic company data enrichment, update the `fields.website` field with a valid company website URL. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

delete.description: Permanently removes a fundraising record from the system.

schema.description:
Filter by stage IDs. Stage IDs can be found in GET /v1/fundraisings/stages endpoint.

**Note:** To provide multiple values, repeat the parameter in query: `?stages=value1&stages=value2`
Search query string to filter results.
Maximum number of results to return per request.
Number of results to skip for pagination.

200.description: List of fundraisings matching the search criteria

get.description: Returns **fundraisings** based on search criteria, with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

post.description:
Creates a new fundraising record in the specified stage. The fundraising will be associated with the authenticated organization.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To enable automatic company data enrichment, provide a valid company website URL in the `fields.website` field. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

get.description: Returns the full folder and list hierarchy. Each item has a `type` field: if `type` is `FOLDER`, the item contains `folderName` and `inside` (an array of nested items). If `type` is `LIST`, the item contains `listId` and `listName`.

200.description: Schema of custom fields for contact

get.description: Returns the schema of all custom fields configured for the Contact entity. Custom fields allow organizations to extend the default contact data model with additional attributes.

schema.description: Search query string to filter results.
Maximum number of results to return per request.
Number of results to skip for pagination.

get.description: Return **contacts** based on search criteria.

post.description: Creates a new contact in the specified list. If contact information such as emails or social URLs (e.g. LinkedIn) are provided, an automatic enrichment check will be triggered to populate additional contact details from external data sources.

get.description: Returns a single **contact** by its unique identifier.

patch.description: Partially updates a contact by its unique identifier. Only provided fields are modified. **Note:** `jobs`, `location`, and `homeLocation` are fully replaced when provided. `socialLinks` is partially updated — only the provided sub-fields are overwritten.

delete.description: Permanently removes a contact by its unique identifier.

post.description: Adds a new **custom field** to the contact schema.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **custom field** in the contact schema. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **custom field** from the contact schema.

post.description: Uploads a photo for a contact. Send the file as `multipart/form-data` with the field name `file`. Replaces any existing photo.

delete.description: Removes the photo from a contact.
Removes a contact from the specified list only. The contact remains in other lists it belongs to. To permanently delete a contact from all lists, use `DELETE /v1/contacts/:id` instead.

200.description: Schema of custom fields for investors

get.description: **Custom fields** allow users to store additional information

post.description: Adds a new **custom field** to the investor schema.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **custom field** in the investor schema. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **custom field** from the investor schema.

get.description: Returns a single **investor** by its unique identifier.

patch.description:
Updates an existing **investors** in the system.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To enable automatic company data enrichment, provide a valid company website URL in the `fields.website` field. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **investors** from the system.

schema.description: Search query string to filter results.
Maximum number of results to return per request.
Number of results to skip for pagination.

200.description: List of investors matching the search criteria

get.description: Returns **investors** based on search criteria, with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

post.description:
Creates a new **investors** in the system.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To enable automatic company data enrichment, provide a valid company website URL in the `fields.website` field. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

get.description: Returns all **note folders** accessible to the authenticated user.

schema.description: Filter notes by folder ID.
Search query string to filter results.
Maximum number of results to return per request.
Number of results to skip for pagination.

200.description: List of notes matching the search criteria

get.description: Returns **notes** based on search criteria, with optional folder filtering and pagination.

post.description: Creates a new **note** with the specified title, optional text content, and optional folder assignment.

get.description: Returns a single **note** by its unique identifier.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **note**. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified; all other fields remain unchanged.

delete.description: Permanently removes a **note** from the system.

200.description: List of notes linked to the deal

get.description: Returns all **notes** linked to the specified deal.

post.description: Establishes a link between an existing **note** and a **deal**. No request body is needed.

delete.description: Removes the link between a **note** and a **deal**. The note itself is not deleted.

200.description: List of notes linked to the investor

get.description: Returns all **notes** linked to the specified investor.

post.description: Establishes a link between an existing **note** and an **investor**. No request body is needed.

delete.description: Removes the link between a **note** and an **investor**. The note itself is not deleted.

200.description: List of notes linked to the fundraising

get.description: Returns all **notes** linked to the specified fundraising.

post.description: Establishes a link between an existing **note** and a **fundraising**. No request body is needed.

delete.description: Removes the link between a **note** and a **fundraising**. The note itself is not deleted.

200.description: List of notes linked to the contact

get.description: Returns all **notes** linked to the specified contact.

post.description: Establishes a link between an existing **note** and a **contact**. No request body is needed.

delete.description: Removes the link between a **note** and a **contact**. The note itself is not deleted.

get.description: Returns a list of users in the organization associated with the API key.

post.description: Creates a new user in the organization associated with the API key.

get.description: Returns a list of teams in the organization associated with the API key.

post.description: Changes the status of a user (ACTIVE or BLOCKED).
Changes the company role of a user.

400.description: Invalid module name or request parameters

post.description: Enables or disables an organization module. Available modules: `deals`, `fundraising`, `investors`, `fees`, `themes`, `companies`, `contacts`.

200.description: Schema of custom fields for companies

get.description: **Custom fields** allow users to store additional information on companies.

post.description: Adds a new **custom field** to the company schema.

patch.description: Partially updates an existing **custom field** in the company schema. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Deletes an existing **custom field** from the company schema.

get.description: Returns all available **company lists** for the authenticated organization.

post.description: Creates a new **company list** for the authenticated organization.

patch.description: Updates an existing **company list**. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified.

delete.description: Permanently removes a **company list**. Companies in the list are not deleted, but their association with this list is removed.

schema.description: Filter companies by list ID.
Search query string to filter results.
Maximum number of results to return per request.
Number of results to skip for pagination.

200.description: List of companies matching the search criteria

get.description: Return **companies** based on search criteria, with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

post.description:
Creates a new company in the system. The company will be associated with the authenticated organization.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To enable automatic company data enrichment, provide a valid company website URL in the `website` field. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

get.description: Returns a single **company** by its unique identifier.

patch.description:
Partially updates an existing company. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified; all other fields remain unchanged.

**Auto-Enrichment:** To trigger automatic company data enrichment, provide a valid company website URL in the `website` field. When a valid URL is supplied, the system will automatically populate additional company information.

delete.description: Permanently removes a company from the system.

post.description: Adds a **company** to the specified **list**.

delete.description: Removes a **company** from the specified **list**.

tags.name: Deals
tags.description: A **deal** represents the process of acquiring a company. Every deal on the platform belongs to a **pipeline** and is associated with a specific **stage** within that pipeline. The Deal entity supports configurable **custom fields**.

tags.name: Fundraisings
tags.description: A **fundraising** represents an intent to raise capital from existing or prospective investors. Every fundraising on the platform belongs to specific **stage**. The Fundraising entity supports configurable **custom fields**.

tags.name: Companies
tags.description: A **company** is a legal entity tracked on the platform. The Company entity supports configurable **custom fields**.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: A **contact** entity stores details used for communication.

tags.name: Investors
tags.description: A **investor** represents an entity used for tracking relationships, interactions and additional data.

tags.name: Deals-Linking
tags.description: Endpoints for managing associations between deals and related entities such as notes.

tags.name: Fundraisings-Linking
tags.description: Endpoints for managing associations between fundraisings and related entities such as notes.

tags.name: Contacts-Linking
tags.description: Endpoints for managing associations between contacts and related entities such as notes.

tags.name: Investors-Linking
tags.description: Endpoints for managing associations between investors and related entities such as notes.

tags.name: Deal Attachments
tags.description: Upload and manage file attachments for deals — both entity-level files and custom fields of type `attachment`.

tags.name: Investor Attachments
tags.description: Upload and manage file attachments for investors — both entity-level files and custom fields of type `attachment`.

tags.name: Company Attachments
tags.description: Upload and manage file attachments for companies — both entity-level files and custom fields of type `attachment`.

tags.name: Fundraising Attachments
tags.description: Upload and manage file attachments for fundraisings — both entity-level files and custom fields of type `attachment`.
